vBRIEFInfo.author: agent:claude-opus-4.8
vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF: integrate the National Weather Service alerts API to show active thunderstorm watches/warnings on the ride screen (closes #28)

narratives.Overview: Riders need conspicuous notice of active thunderstorm watches and warnings. Open-Meteo (our current provider) only exposes forecast weather codes, not official alerts, so this scope adds a National Weather Service alerts integration (api.weather.gov) and surfaces an active thunderstorm watch/warning on the main ride screen with a lightning-bolt icon and text.
narratives.Problem: The app shows temperature and rain timing but gives no warning when a severe thunderstorm watch or warning is active for the rider's location — exactly the condition a motorcyclist most needs to know about.
narratives.Goals:
1. Query the free NWS active-alerts API for the rider's coordinate.
2. Detect active thunderstorm watches and warnings and pick the most severe.
3. Show a conspicuous lightning-bolt indicator with text on the main screen while one is active, and clear it when none is active.
narratives.FunctionalRequirements:
FR-1: The app MUST query NWS active alerts for the current coordinate using a compliant User-Agent header.
FR-2: It MUST identify thunderstorm watches vs. warnings from the alert event, ignoring canceled alerts and non-watch/warning statements.
FR-3: When multiple thunderstorm alerts are active, a WARNING MUST take visual priority over a WATCH.
FR-4: An active alert MUST render a conspicuous lightning-bolt icon and the alert text on the ride screen; when none is active, nothing thunderstorm-related is shown.
FR-5: Alerts MUST refresh periodically while foregrounded and stop when backgrounded, consistent with the existing weather flow. NWS is US-only; outside the US no alerts are expected and the indicator stays hidden.
narratives.Architecture: Add a pure ThunderstormAlert model (level: watch|warning, event text) plus an OpenMeteo-style mapper over the NWS GeoJSON payload (NWSAlertResponse) that selects the most severe active thunderstorm alert. Add an AlertProvider protocol and NWSAlertService (URLSession, api.weather.gov/alerts/active?point=lat,lon, User-Agent + geo+json Accept). Add a ThunderstormAlertController (ObservableObject) mirroring WeatherController: periodic refresh, location updates, start/stop. Wire it through AppModel and display via a ThunderstormAlertBanner in RideView.
narratives.TestingStrategy: Unit-test the NWS mapper: warning beats watch, watch detected, canceled alerts ignored, non-thunderstorm/non-watch-warning events ignored, empty payload -> nil. Unit-test NWSAlertService URL construction and User-Agent header, and decoding via a stubbed URLProtocol. Unit-test ThunderstormAlertController with a fake provider (fetch on first location fix, no fetch without location). Banner presentation verified by build + on-device review.

narrative.Acceptance: A pure mapper turns an NWS payload into an optional ThunderstormAlert, preferring warning over watch and ignoring canceled / non-watch-warning events, covered by unit tests.

NWSAlertService requests api.weather.gov/alerts/active for a coordinate with a compliant User-Agent, decodes the response, and returns the active thunderstorm alert; URL/header/decoding covered by stubbed tests.
Controller fetches on first location fix, refreshes periodically while running, stops cleanly, and publishes the current alert; covered by fake-provider tests.
RideView shows a conspicuous lightning-bolt banner with the alert text when active (warning more severe than watch) and nothing when inactive.
